mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are not significantly negative when FEDFUNDS increases.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate crossing above 5.0 percent signals labor market deterioration that immediately reduces consumer spending expectations, triggering negative equity returns within one week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 60 percent of instances when UNRATE exceeds 5.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate broad-based demand contraction that reduces corporate revenue forecasts, driving negative equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict negative 60-day returns with statistical significance (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, a sustained decline in industrial production signals capacity utilization stress and margin compression, leading to negative equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening periods do not correlate with negative 20-day forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 3.5 percent, inflation expectations force markets to price in aggressive central bank tightening, generating negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL readings above 3.5 percent are followed by positive or neutral 5-day returns in the majority of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence where total nonfarm payrolls rise while the unemployment rate simultaneously increases indicates labor force participation expansion rather than job destruction, supporting consumer spending and generating positive equity returns.
failure_condition: PAYEMS/UNRATE divergence fails to predict positive 20-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth directly increases corporate profit margins and dividend payouts, producing positive forward equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth shows no positive correlation with 60-day returns over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production falls below its 12-month moving average, it signals an impending supply-side contraction that reduces earnings forecasts and drives negative equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: INDPRO crossing below its 12-month MA does not precede negative 20-day returns in more than 55 percent of historical instances.
